structure: PRD.md has sections: Problem Statement, Scope, User Stories, Acceptance Criteria
semantic: Every user story has at least one acceptance criterion
Scope clearly states in-scope and out-of-scope items
Success metrics are measurable (numbers, thresholds, or observable outcomes)

structure: TECH-DESIGN.md has sections: Architecture, Components, Data Flow, Risks
semantic: Every user story from PRD is traced to a component or subsystem
Risks section identifies at least one mitigation per risk
Design does not contradict any documented tech-stack constraints (docs/its/)

structure: TEST-PLAN.md has sections: Scope, Test Cases, Coverage Matrix
Every PRD acceptance criterion maps to at least one test case
semantic: Test cases cover happy path, edge cases, and error paths
Coverage matrix shows no gaps against acceptance criteria

structure: Commits on branch reference the epic key
semantic: No TODO/FIXME left in changed files without an issue link
Reviewer called out deviations from TECH-DESIGN if any

structure: TEST-SCRIPT.md lists steps to reproduce each user story
semantic: Steps reference actual screens/components from implementation
Expected outcomes match PRD acceptance criteria

structure: Git tag exists for the epic commits
semantic: Release notes reference the epic key and user-facing changes

structure: Health/metric report exists for post-release window
semantic: Metrics reflect the acceptance criteria from PRD
Relevant docs/core-business/*.md files reflect the shipped behavior
